title: quercus lanuginosa var. rufa
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. rufa, also known as the red-barked Chinese cork tree or Chinese corkbark, is a variety of evergreen tree belonging to the beech family, characterized by its reddish-brown bark and edible leaves. The leaves and bark of Quercus lanuginosa var. rufa possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus lanuginosa var. rufa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, making it a valuable herb in traditional Chinese medicine. Quercus lanuginosa var. rufa is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine formulations to harness its medicinal properties.
